description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a hip roof and a covered front porch supported by four columns. The exterior is finished in a muted gray color with white trim around the windows, doors, and roof edges. The house features symmetrical window arrangements on both floors, with double-hung windows on the sides and a larger set of windows above the porch at the front. The porch area is spacious and elevated slightly above ground level, providing a sheltered entryway. The roof is steeply pitched and extends over the porch, creating a cohesive and classic silhouette. The overall design is simple yet elegant, reminiscent of American suburban homes.
description: A two-story gray house with a hip roof, white trim, and a covered front porch supported by columns. Symmetrical windows and a spacious entryway give it a classic suburban appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing two sets of double-hung windows on each floor and the edge of the covered porch.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entryway, covered porch with columns, and a large window set above the porch.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a similar window arrangement as the first side, and a partial view of the porch roof.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain wall with no visible windows or doors, and the hip roof structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with four columns, elevated entry, and a simple door beneath a roof overhang.
